summary: Large decompilation/matching PR for Kirby copied neutral-special logic in src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNPk.c. It replaced many placeholder markers with C implementations for Pikachu/Pichu collision transitions, Koopa/Giga Bowser flame handling, Link/Young Link bow/arrow state handling, and Samus charge-shot handling. Supporting header fixes changed two Link helper prototypes from void to bool, exposed ftKb_Init_803CB540, and corrected ftKb_DatAttrs::specialn_ss_frames_per_charge_level from u32 to s32. The author noted one remaining unresolved register-swap issue in ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim; decomp.dev reported +10360 matched code bytes, 44 new matches, and ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim improved to 97.36%.
what_changed: Implemented many previously unmatched functions in ftKb_SpecialNPk.c, including ftKb_PkSpecialAirN_Coll, ftKb_SpecialNKp_800FA588, Koopa air/ground collision helpers, Link/Young Link bow helpers, Link/Young Link start/loop/end Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll functions, and Samus charge-shot helpers.
Added item dependencies needed by the new code, notably it/forward.h and it/items/itkoopaflame.h.

Implemented Koopa flame spawning in ftKb_SpecialNKp_800FA588 using ftKb_Init_803CB540 direction data, HSD_Randi, itKoopaFlame_Spawn, dat attribute offsets/scales, item allocation, and charge-dependent SFX selection.

Implemented Link/Young Link copied neutral-B bow and arrow lifecycle: bow/arrow item creation, charge/release handling, motion-state transitions, anim-rate setup, cleanup paths, and ground/air transition logic.

Implemented Samus copied charge-shot logic, including charge-shot item/effect cleanup helpers, shot firing through it_802B56E4, recoil application in air, charge-level sound selection in ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim, and hold IASA transitions.
Updated ftKb_Init.h prototypes for ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB500 and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB5F4 from void to bool to reflect failure/cleanup signaling used by callers.
Declared extern enum_t ftKb_Init_803CB540[] in ftKb_Init.h for Koopa flame direction lookup.
Changed ftKb_DatAttrs::specialn_ss_frames_per_charge_level from u32 to s32 in types.h.

Reworked several matching-sensitive local variable patterns: used s32 motion-state temporaries instead of FtMotionId in some transitions, split declarations from assignments, introduced small static inline helpers, added PAD_STACK/u8 padding locals, and used explicit float literals.
smart_moves: Kept copied-special variants in shared patterns: switches on fp->fv.kb.hat.kind select the correct Pikachu/Pichu, Koopa/Giga Bowser, and Link/Young Link motion IDs while preserving the same transition skeleton.

Factored repeated Samus charge-shot cleanup into small inline helpers such as ftKb_SpecialNSs_DestroyChargeShot and ftKb_ChargeShot_inline, which both reduced duplication and supported matching-sensitive codegen.
Corrected function return types for Link bow/arrow setup helpers to bool, matching call sites that need to abort and clean up when item spawn fails.
Preserved item_gobj across arrow/charge-shot fire calls before invoking pickup/item cleanup, which is an important behavioral detail visible in the diff.
Used data-attribute fields for item loader IDs, item IDs, max charge, charge speed, recoil, and freefall toggles rather than hardcoding gameplay constants.
Added explicit externs for float/data symbols used to replace literals in matching-sensitive Samus position helper code.
decomp_lessons: For Kirby copied specials, many functions are variant selectors over the copied fighter kind; matching often requires leaving the default motion ID initialized to the base fighter and only changing it for alternate hats.
If a helper is used as a spawn-or-fail predicate, the header prototype must reflect bool return type; otherwise later cleanup/control-flow matching will be misleading.
Ground/air collision transition helpers commonly preserve cur_anim_frame and call ftCommon_8007D5D4 or ftCommon_8007D7FC before Fighter_ChangeMotionState with specific bitflag masks.

Item-spawn/firing functions often require world positions from lb_8000B1CC on specific fighter parts, z-zeroing, saved item_gobj restoration, and damage/death callback setup via a shared Kirby helper.
Small local-layout choices matter: PAD_STACK, dummy u8 arrays, declaration ordering, s32 vs enum typedefs, and inline wrappers were all used in this PR to improve matching.
Signedness in dat attrs can be significant for codegen; specialn_ss_frames_per_charge_level matched as s32 rather than u32.
Remaining near-match problems may reduce to register allocation, as the author specifically called out an unresolved regswap in ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim.
naming_conventions: Kirby copied-special functions retain ftKb_ prefix and encode copied fighter abbreviations in the middle, such as Pk, Kp, Lk, Cl, and Ss.

Unidentified address-suffixed functions remain named with their address, e.g. ftKb_SpecialNKp_800FA588, ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB500, ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB5F4, ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB6DC, ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB880, and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FBA00.

Motion-state names follow ftKb_MS_<copied fighter><Special state> patterns, e.g. ftKb_MS_LkSpecialNStart, ftKb_MS_ClSpecialAirNStart, ftKb_MS_KpSpecialAirNEnd, ftKb_MS_GkSpecialNEnd, ftKb_MS_SsSpecialNHold.

Dat attribute fields use descriptive specialn_<fighter abbreviation>_<purpose> names, such as specialn_lk_bow_item_loader_id, specialn_cl_bow_item_id, specialn_kp_breath_x_offset, specialn_ss_charge_time, and specialn_ss_frames_per_charge_level.
Inline helper names in this PR are descriptive but still local to the file, e.g. ftKb_SpecialNSs_DestroyChargeShot, ftKb_ChargeShot_inline, and facing_to_angle.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK and dummy u8 padding locals to influence stack layout in newly matched functions.
Used small static inline helpers to shape repeated cleanup/recoil/angle code without introducing out-of-line calls.
Changed some FtMotionId locals to s32 to match expected codegen in switch-based motion-state selection.
Separated declaration from assignment in some blocks, especially dat_attrs locals, to adjust register allocation.
Used explicit NULL comparisons and assignments, saved temporary item pointers, and exact float literals such as 0.08726646f, 0.3333f, and 0.6666f where visible in the diff.
Author-reported unresolved tactic: ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim still had a register swap and was not fully matched.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
Author note in PR body: "Couldn't figure out the regswap on ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim 😔".

decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased by 10360 bytes, matched data by 24 bytes, and listed 44 new matches. It specifically reported ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim as improved from 0.00% to 97.36%, not 100%.
follow_up_queries: Look for the later PR that fully matches ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim or resolves its register swap.
Check whether address-named helpers ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB500, ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB5F4, ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB6DC, ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB880, and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FBA00 later received semantic names.
Compare other Kirby copied neutral-special files for the same ground/air collision transition pattern and bool item-spawn helper convention.
Search later changes touching ftKb_Init_803CB540, ftKb_Init_804D9420, ftKb_Init_803CB6C0, or ftKb_Init_804D9448 for data-symbol naming improvements.
